build: Reframed this site around the bot and cut the filler sections.
learned: A portfolio only works if it reflects what you actually build.
impact: The main story now actually maps to a year of real production work.

build: Replaced fake console visuals with a sanitized replay view tied to real incident patterns.
learned: Concrete runtime behavior lands better than abstract polish.
impact: Incident steps, source files, and outcomes are now visible in one panel.

build: Audited the bot repo and updated proof metrics from real file counts.
learned: Exact numbers land better than 'extensive' or 'comprehensive'.
impact: Proof panel now shows real module, scheduler, and test counts.

build: Moved playground details into an in-place workbench with presets and live signal bars.
learned: If interaction requires click + scroll + read, most people won't do it.
impact: Exploration works without leaving the page.

build: Hardened admin auth with role gating, CSRF handling, and timing-safe credential checks.
learned: Security work is mostly just being explicit about things you were previously hoping for.
impact: Dashboard ops are safer without adding friction to the stuff I do every day.

build: Implemented scheduler stale-threshold logic and status escalation (healthy, degraded, critical).
learned: Knowing a scheduler is 'running' isn't enough. It can be alive and completely stale.
impact: Found a scheduler that had been silently stopped for hours. Would not have caught it before.

build: Added cleanup of interval references and long-run process resources on graceful shutdown.
learned: Every restart was leaking something. Intervals, connections, references — none of it was getting cleaned.
impact: Restarts stopped leaving orphaned intervals and connections behind.

build: Stabilized dashboard live-update feed with connection tracking and safer broadcast paths.
learned: WebSocket broadcast without connection tracking turns into noise fast.
impact: Operators can watch runtime state without the feed disconnecting and reconnecting constantly.

build: Added circuit-breaker behavior and fallback data paths for ESPN outages.
learned: ESPN went down during a game window. The bot went dark with it. That was the last time.
impact: Provider went down mid-game and the bot stayed up instead of going dark.

build: Retuned cache TTL strategy by data type — live scores, schedules, and rankings all have different decay rates.
learned: One global TTL is either too stale or too expensive. You have to think per data type.
impact: Score freshness improved. API pressure stayed within safe bounds.

build: Added overlap protection so recurring jobs can't stack if the previous run is still going.
learned: Two instances of the same job stacked when the first ran long. Watched the queue depth climb before I figured it out.
impact: Queue depth stopped climbing during slow runs.

build: Moved heavy processing to Bull-backed workers to protect alert delivery latency.
learned: Backpressure controls matter more than micro-optimizing individual code paths.
impact: Delivery lag dropped during high-volume game windows.

build: Added time-aware windows to ranking update schedulers so they only fire during known release windows.
learned: Schedulers that run at any time create a lot of noise cleanup work.
impact: Ranking updates post during actual release windows instead of randomly.

build: Shipped automated SVG scoreboard rendering. Sharp memory issues showed up immediately.
learned: Sharp doesn't share cache limits across instances — found this the hard way when image memory kept climbing.
impact: Scoreboards ship. Sharp global config now loads before any other module.

build: Built scheduler orchestration and heartbeat tracking across 14 automation loops.
learned: Automations that run in the background with no visibility will fail silently for longer than you'd expect.
impact: Stale and degraded schedulers became detectable before they caused user-facing misses.

build: Added in-memory fallback and periodic health checks for volatile Redis cache paths.
learned: A degraded cache fallback keeps the system running. A hard miss on every request does not.
impact: Partial Redis outages no longer take the whole bot down with them.

build: Added API optimization layer: batching, retry normalization, and per-source rate-limit routing.
learned: Flaky upstream responses used to take the whole delivery path down. Pacing and normalization absorbed most of it.
impact: Flaky ESPN responses stopped cascading into missed alerts.

build: Built event fingerprinting and dedupe rules to stop repeated alert spam.
learned: ESPN event IDs aren't consistent enough to use as naive dedupe keys. Same event, different ID, every time.
impact: Stopped sending the same update 4–5 times during active game windows.

build: Split startup into three phases and separated core modules for clearer failure isolation.
learned: Before this, a boot failure anywhere meant guessing. Phases made it obvious which component died.
impact: Boot failures got locatable. Recovery went from guessing to reading the phase.

build: Added health/status endpoints and internal dashboard route structure.
learned: Debugging a production issue with no observability surface is miserable. Added this after one too many blind restarts.
impact: Can check runtime state without touching the bot directly.

build: Shipped the first end-to-end live ingest and Discord delivery loop on Railway.
learned: Local testing hid most of the real failure modes. Shipping exposed them immediately.
impact: First real delivery. Duplicates and noisy retries showed up within the first game window.
